mnemonics: GRAD to Success: Gradient of a vector-valued function is found by taking partial derivatives with Respect to Each variable, And then writing the result as a vector
keyPoints: The gradient of a vector-valued function is a vector of partial derivatives
It is used to find the maximum or minimum of a function
The gradient points in the direction of the greatest increase of the function
commonMistakes: Forgetting to write the partial derivatives as a vector
Confusing the gradient with the divergence or curl of a vector field
